mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year walk-forward test, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that force immediate equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE spikes > 0.4pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with > 55% accuracy over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and rising input costs, compressing profit margins and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 250.0 indicates entrenched price pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in > 58% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.0, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts household income and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to yield a positive 60-day return in > 50% of observed cases, or the interaction term shows no predictive alpha over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate earnings trajectories, creating a baseline positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns over a 5-year period, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals fragile supply chain normalization that undermines manufacturing output and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines in post-shock periods are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances, or the signal loses predictive power when supply chain stress indices normalize.
